# Pull request

## What does this change?

One paragraph. Focus on the *why*, not the *what* — the diff shows the
what.

## Type of change

- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] ADR (propose / accept / supersede)
- [ ] Provider
- [ ] Core / backend
- [ ] UI / frontend
- [ ] Deployment / compose / CI
- [ ] Security fix
- [ ] Other:

## Linked issues / ADRs

- Closes #
- Related ADR:

## Checklist

- [ ] The change is covered by docs (README, docs/*, or provider
      README). Undocumented behavior is not shipped.
- [ ] Terminology follows [GLOSSARY.md](../docs/GLOSSARY.md).
- [ ] If this introduces or changes a security-relevant surface, I
      have read [SECURITY_MODEL.md](../docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md) and
      updated it if needed.
- [ ] If this touches credentials, I have read
      [CREDENTIALS.md](../docs/CREDENTIALS.md) and plaintext does
      **not** appear in logs, UI, or audit events.
- [ ] I have added or updated tests (where code exists).
- [ ] Commits are signed off (DCO).
- [ ] I am willing to license this contribution under the project's
      current license ([ADR-0004](../docs/adr/0004-license.md)).

## How to test

Concrete reproduction steps a reviewer can follow.

## Screenshots / recordings

For UI changes. Omit for others.

## Notes for reviewers

Anything you specifically want eyes on: tradeoffs, open questions,
out-of-scope items intentionally deferred.
